subject: Ecology
title: Constructed Wetlands
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Constructed Wetlands.

hook.text: Engineers build artificial wetlands to clean water—copying what nature does for free.

buildup.text: Constructed wetlands channel polluted water through planted beds where microbes and roots purify it.

discovery.text: They effectively treat sewage, stormwater, mine drainage, and agricultural runoff.

twist.text: Constructed wetlands cost up to 90% less to operate than conventional treatment plants.

climax.text: Over 50,000 constructed wetlands operate worldwide, treating billions of liters daily.

punchline.text: The best water technology was invented by swamps.

quiz.question: How do constructed wetlands compare in cost to conventional treatment?
